title: Prometheus OOMs due to high cardinality metrics
symptoms: Prometheus pod restarts with OOMKilled every few hours
TSDB head series count exceeds 5M
Queries time out in Grafana
root_cause: A service exposes a label containing request_id or user_id
Recording rules generate an explosion of series
Prometheus retention increased without scaling memory
diagnosis: Container memory usage in Grafana → Prometheus dashboard
fix: Drop the offending label via relabel_config:

```yaml
metric_relabel_configs:
  - source_labels: [request_id]
    action: labeldrop
```
Deploy a remote-write tiered setup (Thanos/Mimir) for long retention
Restart Prometheus after applying the scrape config
prevention: Lint metrics in CI using promtool and a cardinality budget
Add an alert when head_series grows > 20% week-over-week
Enforce a label naming guide in the platform handbook

title: Elasticsearch cluster stuck in yellow after node replacement
symptoms: GET _cluster/health returns status: yellow
unassigned_shards > 0 but no allocation explanation
Kibana dashboards display 'shards failed'
root_cause: New node added with a different attribute (zone) breaking awareness rules
Disk watermark exceeded on one node and shards cannot allocate back
Total shards per node limit reached
fix: Raise the watermark temporarily so recovery can proceed:
Re-route the stuck shard manually if needed:

```json
POST _cluster/reroute?retry_failed=true
```
prevention: Automate node attribute labelling via user data
Track shard-to-node ratio and rebalance before maintenance
Use ILM to keep shards below 50GB each

title: Grafana dashboards showing no data due to datasource errors
symptoms: Dashboards display 'No data' or 'Datasource error' messages
Queries in query editor fail with connection errors
Datasource test shows successful connection but queries fail
Some panels work while others show errors
root_cause: Datasource authentication credentials expired or invalid
Datasource endpoint changed or unreachable
Query syntax incompatible with datasource version
Timeout settings too short for complex queries
diagnosis: Test datasource connection from Grafana UI
Review datasource logs for error messages
fix: Update datasource credentials and connection details
Verify datasource endpoint is accessible from Grafana
Fix query syntax to match datasource API version
Increase query timeout settings in datasource configuration
prevention: Monitor datasource health and set up alerts
Use environment variables for sensitive credentials
Test dashboards after datasource updates
Document datasource configuration and query patterns

title: Loki logs being dropped due to ingestion rate limits
symptoms: Missing log lines in Loki queries
Promtail logs showing rate limit errors
Loki metrics showing dropped log entries
Recent logs not appearing in Grafana
root_cause: Ingestion rate limit exceeded due to log volume spike
Stream limits too low for number of log streams
Burst rate limit too small for log patterns
Unstructured logs creating too many unique streams
diagnosis: Check Loki metrics for rate limit violations
Review Promtail logs for ingestion errors
fix: Increase ingestion rate limit in Loki configuration
Adjust stream limits to accommodate workload
Implement log sampling or filtering to reduce volume
Use structured logging to reduce stream cardinality
prevention: Monitor log volume and adjust limits proactively
Implement structured logging with consistent labels
Use log aggregation and filtering at source
Set up alerts for rate limit violations

title: Traces not appearing in Tempo despite application instrumentation
symptoms: Application sends traces but Tempo shows no data
Tempo UI displays 'No traces found' for queries
Trace ID exists in application but not searchable
Intermittent trace loss affecting some services
root_cause: Tempo ingestion endpoint not reachable from application
Trace sampling rate too low causing traces to be dropped
Batch size or timeout settings misconfigured
Tempo storage backend having issues
diagnosis: Check Tempo ingestion metrics and logs
Test network connectivity to Tempo endpoint
fix: Fix network connectivity to Tempo ingestion endpoint
Increase trace sampling rate in application configuration
Adjust batch size and timeout settings in trace exporter
Check and fix Tempo storage backend issues
prevention: Monitor Tempo ingestion metrics and set up alerts
Test trace export during application deployment
Document trace exporter configuration
Use appropriate sampling rates for production workload

title: Alertmanager not firing alerts due to incorrect silence configuration
symptoms: Critical alerts not being sent despite trigger conditions met
Alerts showing in Prometheus but not in Alertmanager
Silences blocking alerts unexpectedly
Alert routing not working as expected
root_cause: Silence rules too broad matching all alerts
Silence not expired after maintenance window
Alert routing configuration incorrect
Receiver configuration missing or misconfigured
diagnosis: Check active silences in Alertmanager UI
Check Alertmanager logs for routing decisions
fix: Remove or update overly broad silence rules
Set proper expiry times for maintenance silences
Fix alert routing configuration
Verify and test receiver configurations
prevention: Use specific matchers in silence rules
Document silence procedures and expiry policies
Test alert routing after configuration changes
Implement alert management best practices

title: Jaeger collector not receiving spans from agents
symptoms: Jaeger UI showing no traces despite application sending spans
Agent logs showing connection failures to collector
Collector metrics showing zero span ingestion
Traces available locally but not in centralized Jaeger
root_cause: Collector service not running or crashed
Network connectivity between agents and collector blocked
Collector endpoint misconfigured in agents
Collector overwhelmed by span volume
diagnosis: Check collector service status and logs
Verify network connectivity from agents to collector
Review agent configuration for collector endpoint
fix: Restart or fix Jaeger collector service
Fix network connectivity between agents and collector
Update agent configuration with correct collector endpoint
Scale collector resources to handle span volume
prevention: Monitor collector health and set up alerts
Implement collector high availability
Test connectivity after infrastructure changes
Document collector deployment and configuration

title: Vector log pipeline dropping events due to buffer overflow
symptoms: Missing log events in output destinations
Vector metrics showing buffer overflow events
Log loss during traffic spikes
Backpressure causing source slowdown
root_cause: Buffer size too small for incoming event rate
Output sink slower than input source
Network issues blocking output delivery
CPU or memory limitations on Vector instance
diagnosis: Check Vector metrics for buffer usage and overflow
fix: Increase buffer size in Vector configuration
Optimize sink performance or add capacity
Implement batching and compression in sinks
Scale Vector resources or add more instances
prevention: Monitor buffer usage and set up alerts
Right-size buffers based on expected throughput
Implement load testing for log pipelines
Use disk-based buffers for durability

title: Thanos compaction falling behind causing query performance degradation
symptoms: Thanos Store API queries becoming slow
Compaction lag metrics showing increasing delay
High object storage API costs due to uncompacted data
Query timeouts for long time ranges
root_cause: Compaction resources insufficient for data volume
Compaction interval too long for data growth rate
Compaction job not running or failing
Object storage throttling compaction operations
diagnosis: Check Thanos compactor metrics and logs
Monitor compaction lag in Thanos UI
fix: Scale compactor resources to handle data volume
Reduce compaction interval for more frequent runs
Fix compaction job issues and restart if needed
Optimize object storage configuration for compaction workload
prevention: Monitor compaction lag and set up alerts
Right-size compactor based on data growth
Test compaction configuration in staging
Document compaction strategy and retention policies

title: OpenTelemetry exporter failing to send telemetry to backend
symptoms: Application metrics not appearing in backend
OTel collector logs showing export failures
Telemetry data loss during transmission
High memory usage in OTel SDK due to batching
root_cause: Exporter endpoint unreachable or misconfigured
Authentication credentials invalid or expired
Batch size too large causing rejections
Network issues blocking telemetry transmission
diagnosis: Review collector logs for export errors
Monitor SDK metrics for export failures
fix: Fix exporter endpoint configuration
Update authentication credentials
Adjust batch size and timeout settings
Implement retry logic and backoff in exporter
prevention: Monitor exporter success rate and set up alerts
Test telemetry export during deployment
Use environment variables for endpoint configuration
Implement circuit breaker for failing backends
